text: The laparoscopic view shows the kidney from below, with the lower pole closest to the camera. The kidney is slightly rotated posteriorly, and the pelvis is centrally positioned in the surgical field. The hepatic flexure is adhered to the lateral abdominal wall, requiring careful dissection. A vein is visible leading to the lower pole, limiting further mobilization of the pelvis to avoid vascular damage.

The ureter is easily identified in the surgical field, facilitating straightforward dissection. The surgeon begins the PUJ anastomosis using robotic instruments, which maneuver efficiently in the limited space. The robotic fulcrum at the instrument tip allows precise movements, contrasting with the amplified hand movements in laparoscopy due to the abdominal wall fulcrum.

The surgeon demonstrates the range of movement of robotic instruments, performing hand-like suturing on the posterior wall. A stent is being placed, followed by the closure of the anterior wall. The robotic approach provides a different perspective compared to conventional port placement.
